summary: vite-svgr typosquats vite-plugin-svgr and fetches remote code from jsonkeeper.com to execute via new Function
details: The package name impersonates the popular vite-plugin-svgr, but its contents are a fork of tsconfig-paths with an added remote-execution payload. lib/mapProps.js performs an HTTP GET against https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/EQUBH, takes the response's `Cookie` field, and runs it through `new Function("require", s)(require)` — giving the fetched string full `require` access in the consumer's Node process. mapProps.js is reached from the exported `configJson` API in lib/config-loader.js, which spawns it as a detached child with `stdio: "ignore"`, hiding any output from the user. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable public paste host, so the executed code is attacker-mutable at any time. Any installer who mistypes `vite-plugin-svgr` as `vite-svgr` and invokes the API runs whatever JavaScript the paste currently serves.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
